description_long: This is a two-story brick and stucco house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered gray shingles. The front facade features a prominent balcony with a balustrade above the main entrance, flanked by symmetrical windows with decorative lintels. The side walls are lined with tall, evenly spaced windows framed by brick pilasters, giving the structure a stately, historic appearance. The rear and side elevations are simpler, with fewer windows and visible weathering on the stucco surfaces. The house has a wooden door with a cross-brace design and subtle decorative trim under the eaves. The overall look suggests a late 19th to early 20th-century urban townhouse, blending classical and vernacular elements.
description: A two-story brick and stucco townhouse with a steep gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a front balcony above the main entrance. The design features classical details and weathered textures, evoking a historic urban residence.
description_view_0: Side view showing six tall windows framed by brick pilasters and the edge of the front balcony.
description_view_1: Front view with a central wooden door, balcony with balustrade, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first side with six tall windows and visible roof texture.
description_view_3: Rear view with a simpler facade, two windows, and a wooden door, lacking the balcony and ornate details.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with cross-brace, balcony above, balustrade, and decorative trim
